metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the AC prose in child e8d3 from 'the new four included' to 'the new five included' (or 'the five new cases') to match the proof command's enumeration of R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary.
findings.finding: Child e8d3's AC text describes 'the new four' eval cases but the What section and the AC2 proof command enumerate exactly five new case IDs (R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary). The prose 'four' contradicts the operative proof; a completion verifier that reads the AC text before the proof script may flag the mismatch and fail the close gate.
findings.location: child e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae — Acceptance Criteria, fifth bullet: 'the existing spec-validation tests, which assert every dataset case (the new four included)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the new four included' to 'the new five included' in the fifth AC bullet of child e8d3. The count of five is the correct value: three recall cases (R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam) plus two no-fire cases (FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a diff-readable litmus to the `unrealistic fault injection` antipattern (analogous to the inverted-trace disproof for tautological tests) that specifies how the reviewer determines seam position — e.g., 'the patched target is the seam under test iff it is the function whose failure the contract explicitly covers (cite the recovery/rollback clause from the contract); patching any caller above that function is a wrong-seam injection.'
findings.finding: The `unrealistic fault injection — wrong seam` recall case (R-fault-wrong-seam) requires the reviewer to determine that a monkeypatched target is a 'top-level wrapper' rather than 'the real boundary', but no decision rule or diff-readable litmus is specified in the plan for how the overlay makes this seam-position judgment. The plan defines the antipattern conceptually but omits the inference rule needed at inference time.
findings.location: Task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae description, 'Antipatterns to add to the FLAG list — unrealistic fault injection' + recall case R-fault-wrong-seam

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add {path: 'src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml', reason: 'add five new eval cases (3 recall + 2 no-fire) for non-gating and unrealistic-fault antipatterns'} to child e8d3's file_impact array.
findings.finding: Child e8d3's ticket file_impact metadata lists only src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md but omits src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml, which is a required deliverable per the child's own What section, AC2 (eval case IDs proof), and AC5 (pytest passes with new eval cases present).
findings.location: child e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae — file_impact array in ticket metadata
findings.suggested_fix: Append to file_impact in ticket e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae: {"path": "src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml", "reason": "add five new eval cases (R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary) for the two new antipatterns"}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended copy mechanism (e.g., 'symlinks checked into the repo' or 'a build script copies canonical → per-skill at test time') so the executor does not have to design this.
findings.finding: The mechanism for producing 'byte-identical per-skill copies' is unspecified — symlinks, build-time generated copies, a CI script, or manually maintained files are all plausible and have materially different maintenance implications. An executor must make a real design decision the plan defers.
findings.location: Decomposition, Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900: 'canonical file + byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated by a unit test'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Decomposition: 'Per-skill copies are symlinks pointing to shared/test-design.md; the drift-gating unit test verifies that all three symlink targets resolve to the same inode/content as the canonical file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive content outline for test-design.md (e.g., required section headings or a bulleted list of the specific rules each section must encode) so reviewers can evaluate completeness without re-deriving it from the gap list.
findings.finding: The required content and structure of test-design.md are described only negatively (via the gaps the document must fill) with no positive specification of sections, rules, or length. An executor knows the six problems to address but not how to structure the authoritative standard, leaving the document's actual content unconstrained.
findings.location: Context paragraph: 'no existing-coverage inventory before authoring, no test-tier selection rule, no precondition/collateral-invariant oracle guidance, no fault-realism rules, no proof the test gates CI'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that verifies the drift-gating unit test exists and passes, e.g., 'CI log for the merged change shows the drift-gate test suite passing with ≥1 test covering per-skill copy parity.'
findings.finding: The 'drift-gated by a unit test' deliverable stated in the Decomposition has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion — AC1 only checks that the task's Gerrit change is merged, not that the drift-gating test was written and is green.
findings.location: Decomposition (Task 43c9) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'and the CI run for that change shows a passing drift-gate test (e.g., `pytest tests/test_skill_copy_parity.py` exits 0).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) to explicitly verify that rebar-debug's llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md is updated to defer Phase 2 RED-artifact wording to the shared test-design standard.
findings.finding: The Decomposition for Task 43c9 names 'rebar-debug's llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md' as an explicit deliverable, but AC1 only covers 'the three SKILL.md integrations' — the taxonomy file edit has no covering acceptance criterion.
findings.location: Decomposition (Task 43c9) vs AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1: '…and rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md Phase 2 RED-artifact wording updated to reference shared/test-design.md is Verified +1 and merged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) to verify that the examples README is updated to reference examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md.
findings.finding: The Decomposition for Task 43c9 lists 'the examples README' as a deliverable, but no acceptance criterion covers this update.
findings.location: Decomposition (Task 43c9) vs Acceptance Criteria (all four items)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: '…and the examples/claude-skills/README is updated to reference the shared test-design standard.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the copy-drift unit test for shared/test-design.md exists and passes (e.g., CI green on the new test, or an explicit grep/checksum assertion in the AC).
findings.finding: The Decomposition for Task 43c9 describes a drift-gate unit test ('byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated by a unit test') as a deliverable, but no AC verifies the unit test exists or passes — AC4's mention of 'prompt-index drift' refers to a different, pre-existing CI check.
findings.location: Decomposition (Task 43c9) vs AC4 / Success Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC5: 'A unit test asserting byte-identity between examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md and each per-skill copy is present in the repo and passes in CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Decomposition (or Success Criteria) where rebar-brainstorm's SKILL.md should reference shared/test-design.md, given that its only current testing touchpoint is the plan-review gate.
findings.finding: The Context states 'rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs' (implying no TDD integration point), yet the Success Criteria states the shared reference is 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing' — if brainstorm has no such point, the success criterion is vacuously satisfied or requires a new integration point that is not described.
findings.location: Context paragraph vs Success Criteria bullet 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out/rollback plan for the code-review-tests.md antipattern additions (e.g., a named revert commit strategy or a feature-flag/disabled-by-default staging step) so reviewers know the reversibility mechanism if false-positive guards prove insufficient post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan adds two new antipatterns to src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md, which is a behavioral change for existing consumers: code that previously passed review may now be flagged. While false-positive guards are mentioned, the plan states no explicit rollback/back-out path if the guards prove insufficient after merge.
findings.location: Task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae description; Success Criteria bullet 3; AC bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to the AC or Decomposition section: 'If post-merge monitoring shows new antipatterns firing on >N% of legitimate reviews, the back-out is: git revert <merge SHA of e8d3 change> without touching the eval suite; re-open the task to tighten guards before re-landing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the 'edit canonical (shared/test-design.md), never the per-skill copies' maintenance contract is documented — e.g., a header comment in the canonical file itself and/or an examples/README section — so contributors understand the pattern without reading the plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new maintenance contract — byte-identical per-skill copies of test-design.md managed by a drift-gate unit test — but does not specify where this contributor-facing maintenance pattern is documented, leaving future editors without guidance on which copy is authoritative to edit.
findings.location: Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description: 'canonical file + byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated by a unit test'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the canonical file's preamble: '<!-- CANONICAL SOURCE — do not edit per-skill copies directly; run the drift-gate test after updating this file. Per-skill copies are auto-synced. -->' and add one sentence to the examples README explaining the shared/ directory convention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the canonical-shared-file + byte-identical-copies + drift-gate-unit-test pattern (what it is, how to extend it for future shared assets) in CLAUDE.md or examples/claude-skills/README so future contributors can follow it without reading this plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — canonical shared file + byte-identical per-skill copies + drift-gate unit test — under examples/claude-skills/shared/, but does not designate any location (CLAUDE.md, examples/README, or an ADR) where this pattern is documented for future contributors who need to add or modify shared skill assets.
findings.location: Decomposition, Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900: 'create the shared test-design standard at examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md (canonical file + byte-identical per-skill copies, drift-gated by a unit test)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Contributing shared assets' section to examples/claude-skills/README (or CLAUDE.md if that is the project's contributor guide) explaining: (1) shared/ holds canonical content, (2) per-skill copies must be byte-identical, (3) the drift-gate test name/location and how to regenerate copies.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ACs for (a) the drift-gating unit test (verify it exists and is CI-green, e.g., `pytest` run of the new test passes), (b) the examples README integration (verify the shared reference appears), and (c) the llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md edit (verify the Phase 2 RED-artifact wording is updated).
findings.finding: Several named deliverables from task 43c9 — the drift-gating unit test, the examples README edit, and the llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md edit — appear in the task description but have no corresponding Acceptance Criterion that verifies their existence or CI-green status. The drift-gating unit test is load-bearing (it is the only mechanism ensuring byte-identical per-skill copies stay in sync), yet its presence and passage in CI is not observable from any AC.
findings.location: Decomposition section (task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description) vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] `pytest` (or equivalent) run of the drift-gating unit test passes in CI, confirming byte-identical copy invariant for examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md.
- [ ] git diff of the landed change shows examples/README.md and rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md both edited (grep or diff check analogous to AC3).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism for the skill-side improvement, such as a dogfooding note (e.g., a before/after comparison of a sample skill invocation with and without the standard) or an example-driven acceptance check confirming the shared standard is referenced at the correct integration point in each SKILL.md.
findings.finding: The skill-side improvement (shared test-design standard guiding better test selection in the three skills) has no concrete before/after validation mechanism beyond file presence and Gerrit merge. Unlike the code-review side — which gains recall + no-fire eval cases — the skill side provides no signal that the new guidance actually changes agent behaviour or test quality.
findings.location: Success Criteria and Acceptance Criteria (skill-side, task 43c9)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the Task 43c9 description (or the epic Context section) explaining why a single shared canonical file is preferred over equivalent inline prose in each SKILL.md — e.g. single-source-of-truth eliminates per-skill prose drift and makes adding a future skill a one-step sync rather than a parallel authoring exercise.
findings.finding: The plan states why symlinks were rejected and why the same-directory companion-file pattern was adopted, but never states a positive rationale for having a shared canonical file at all rather than expanding each SKILL.md in-place with equivalent inline test-design prose. The core architectural choice — one shared source vs. three independent inline blocks — is asserted without a stated justification (e.g. maintainability, single-source-of-truth, future-skill extensibility).
findings.location: Decomposition / Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description — 'Reference mechanism' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Reference mechanism' paragraph of task 43c9, prepend: 'A shared canonical file is preferred over equivalent inline prose in each SKILL.md because it provides a single source of truth — per-skill prose blocks drift independently over time (as the rebar-debug/rebar-implement TDD sections already diverge), and a new skill can adopt the standard by syncing one file rather than re-authoring the full guidance.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete litmus to the `FP-sleep-as-secondary` guard specifying how the reviewer identifies a deterministic primary mechanism in the diff — e.g., 'the primary mechanism is deterministic if the same test body (or a visible helper it calls) contains an explicit synchronization primitive (threading.Event.wait, multiprocessing.Barrier.wait, os.pipe read, marker-file poll loop, or equivalent); a sleep is secondary iff such a primitive is present; flag only when the sleep is the sole synchronization artifact visible in the diff.'
findings.finding: The `FP-sleep-as-secondary` guard requires the reviewer to classify a `time.sleep` as a 'secondary settling wait' vs. 'primary synchronization' from diff text, but no decision rule or litmus is specified for this distinction — the plan only names examples of primary mechanisms (barrier, pipe, marker file, event) without a rule for identifying them in a diff.
findings.location: Task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae description, 'Guards' for unrealistic fault injection + no-fire case FP-sleep-as-secondary

findings.finding: The plan names sleep-only synchronization as an independently flaggable sub-variant of the unrealistic-fault-injection antipattern but specifies only a no-fire eval case (FP-sleep-as-secondary) for it and no recall case. The eval suite therefore cannot demonstrate the rubric fires on a pure sleep-only race test; if the rubric text is too narrow or misconditioned, no case will catch it.
findings.location: Task e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae description — 'Eval coverage to add' section; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to code-review-tests.eval.yaml under the unrealistic-fault-injection family:
```yaml
- id: R-sleep-only-race
  corpus: dso
  expect: finding
  mode: unrealistic-fault-injection
  diff: |
    --- a/tests/test_worker.py
    +++ b/tests/test_worker.py
    @@ -1,3 +1,9 @@
    +def test_concurrent_write_no_conflict():
    +    results = []
    +    t = threading.Thread(target=lambda: results.append(worker.process()))
    +    t.start()
    +    # primary synchronization is a bare sleep — no barrier, pipe, marker, or event
    +    time.sleep(0.05)
    +    assert len(results) == 1
```

findings.finding: The `R-non-gating-skip` recall eval case will be permanently suppressed by the reviewer's own fail-open guard, because the eval solver for code-review prompts (confirmed in eval_solver.py lines 182–187) sends the diff inline without a fixture repo — the reviewer's file tools cannot read any CI config file. The reviewer's stated guard ('when the CI context is not determinable, do NOT flag') will always fire, making the case's expected output (a finding) unreachable.
findings.location: Plan 'Eval coverage to add' section / R-non-gating-skip case; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 182-187
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to the R-non-gating-skip case: `files: {'.github/workflows/ci.yml': 'jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps: [...]'}` and use a skip condition like `skipif(sys.platform == 'win32', reason='Windows only')` so the reviewer can read CI config and determine the condition holds.

findings.finding: Child 43c9's RED-evidence requirement for test_skill_shared_sync.py is a partial-without-remainder: the CI gate verifies only the GREEN pass; the RED pre-condition (perturbing a copy, confirming failure, restoring) is verified solely by a human-attested ticket comment with no automated enforcement path.
findings.location: child 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 — Acceptance Criteria: 'tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py asserts byte-identity … FAILS when a copy diverges or is missing (verified RED once by perturbing a copy, then restored)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parametrized fixture in test_skill_shared_sync.py itself that writes a perturbed copy to a tmp_path and asserts the comparison function returns non-zero, so the failure path is exercised within the same automated test run.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
